title: Dolphin Watching Boat Hire in Marbella: Costs and Tips
html:
Dolphin watching boat hire in Marbella starts from Puerto Banús marina, where you can rent a small motorboat for short morning runs along the coast. Day prices typically range from EUR 90 for a shared seat to EUR 1,200 for a crewed yacht, and BoatHire24 lists verified options across the bay.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most dolphin trips leave from a handful of well-marked harbours near the town centre. Each gives quick access to the open Alboran Sea, where common and striped dolphins feed in the warmer months.

  • Puerto Banús: the busiest marina, with the widest choice of motorboats and crewed yachts.
  • Puerto Deportivo de Marbella: the central harbour, handy for shorter hourly hires close to the old town.
  • Puerto de Cabopino: a quieter eastern marina near the Artola dunes, good for calmer family departures.
  • La Bajadilla (Puerto Pesquero): the working fishing port, often used by traditional tour boats.
  • San Pedro de Alcántara: a short drive west, with sheltered launch points toward Estepona.

What is the best boat to rent in Marbella for dolphins?

A small open motorboat or a mid-size motor yacht is best, because both move quietly and let you idle near the pods without crowding them. Speed matters less than a steady, low-wake approach, so choose comfort over horsepower. The list below covers the most common options on the bay.

  1. RIB or open dayboat best for fast, agile spotting on calm mornings, seats 4 to 8 guests, from EUR 60 per hour.
  2. Small motorboat (no licence) best for first-timers staying inshore, seats up to 5 guests, from EUR 90 per half-day.
  3. Motor yacht best for stable, shaded cruising in comfort, such as the the Fairline Targa 43 listing, seats up to 11 guests, from EUR 700 per day.
  4. Sailing catamaran best for slow, quiet drifting and swimming stops, seats up to 12 guests, from EUR 600 per day.
  5. Crewed luxury yacht best for full-day groups wanting a skipper and host, seats 10 or more, from EUR 1,200 per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Costs depend on the boat, the season and whether a skipper is included. As a rough guide for the bay, you can use the bands below when you compare a quick boat hire Marbella against a full crewed charter.

  • A shared dolphin tour seat costs from EUR 30 to EUR 45 per person.
  • A small motorboat for a half-day costs from EUR 90 to EUR 180 in EUR.
  • A sailing catamaran for a full day costs from EUR 600 to EUR 950.
  • A crewed yacht for a full day costs from EUR 1,200 upward, fuel often extra.

When is the best season to watch dolphins in Marbella?

The high season runs from May to September, when warm, settled water brings dolphins closest to the coast and trips run most reliably. Early mornings tend to be calmest, before the sea breeze builds. The Levante, the easterly wind, can chop up the surface, so check it before you book.

Local tip: book the first slot of the day in July or August. The water off Marbella is glassiest soon after sunrise, and dolphins often feed within two to three nautical miles of Puerto Banús before the Levante wind picks up by midday.

On licences, Spain follows the common European principle that small low-power boats can be hired without a certificate, while larger or faster craft need a qualified skipper. The operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat at the time of booking, so always ask before you pay.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 30 per seat to EUR 1,200 per day
  • Peak months: May to September
  • Typical trip length: 1 to 4 hours for dolphin runs
  • Capacity: 4 to 12 guests on most day boats
  • Currency: EUR
  • Common departure: Puerto Banús, Marbella

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Are you guaranteed to see dolphins?

Sightings are never guaranteed because dolphins are wild animals, but common and striped dolphins are seen on most summer mornings, especially within a few nautical miles of the Marbella coast.

How long is a typical dolphin trip?

Most dolphin-watching hires last between one and four hours, with two hours being the popular choice. Shorter hourly slots suit families, while a half-day gives more time to reach the feeding grounds offshore.

Do I need a licence to hire a small boat?

Many small, low-power boats in Marbella can be hired without any licence under Spanish rules, while larger or faster vessels require a skipper. The operator confirms the precise requirement for your chosen boat before booking.

Is BoatHire24 a marketplace or an operator?

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own. It connects you with local owners and operators rather than running the boats directly, so you book verified listings near Puerto Banús.

For dolphin watching in 2026, compare verified motorboats and crewed yachts near Puerto Banús, then book the boat that suits your group and budget. If you own a boat on the Costa del Sol, you can also list it on BoatHire24 and reach travellers searching for trips along the bay.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
